title: Formspec Registry Document
description: A static JSON document format for publishing, discovering, and validating Formspec extensions and semantic metadata. A Registry Document enumerates named entries — custom data types, functions, constraints, properties, namespaces, concept identities, and vocabulary bindings — with metadata, version history, compatibility bounds, and machine-readable schemas. Any organization MAY publish its own Registry Document. Interoperability is achieved through the common format, not centralized authority.

x-lm.intent: Version pin for registry document compatibility.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URI for editor validation and autocompletion.

publisher.description: Organization publishing this registry document. Provides provenance and contact information for all entries unless overridden at the entry level.

x-lm.intent: Publisher identity and contact provenance for this registry.

published.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when this registry version was published. Used for freshness checks and conditional GET cache validation.

x-lm.intent: Publication timestamp used for freshness and cache validation.

entries.description: Array of extension registry entries. Each entry describes one extension with its category, version, compatibility bounds, and category-specific metadata. Within a single document, the (name, version) tuple MUST be unique.

examples.name: x-acme-currency
examples.description: Custom currency data type extending decimal with 2-digit precision.

examples.name: x-acme-fiscal-year
examples.description: Returns the federal fiscal year for a given date.

examples.name: x-acme-duns-check
examples.description: Validates that a string is a well-formed DUNS number (9 digits, valid check digit).

examples.name: x-acme-grants
examples.description: Namespace grouping federal grants management extensions.

examples.name: x-onto-ein
examples.description: IRS Employer Identification Number — a 9-digit tax identifier assigned to business entities.

examples.name: x-vocab-icd10-mental-health
examples.description: ICD-10-CM mental and behavioural disorders chapter (F00-F99), depth-limited to 3 levels.

x-lm.intent: Primary catalog of extension definitions exposed by the registry.

extensions.description: Registry-level extension properties for vendor-specific metadata. All property keys MUST be x-prefixed.

Publisher.description: Identity and contact information for the organization publishing extensions.

contact.description: Contact email address or URI for extension-related inquiries.

RegistryEntry.description: A single registry entry. Describes one Formspec extension or semantic metadata entry — its identity, category, lifecycle status, compatibility bounds, and category-specific metadata. Category-specific properties become required based on the category value: dataType requires baseType; function requires parameters and returns; constraint requires parameters; concept requires conceptUri; vocabulary requires vocabularySystem.

name.description: The x-prefixed extension identifier. Must be lowercase ASCII with hyphen-separated segments, each starting with a letter. The prefix x-formspec- is reserved for future core promotion.

x-lm.intent: Globally unique extension identifier used for resolution and collision avoidance.

category.description: The entry category. Determines which category-specific properties are required: dataType requires baseType; function requires parameters and returns; constraint requires parameters; concept requires conceptUri; vocabulary requires vocabularySystem; property and namespace have no additional required properties.

x-lm.intent: Discriminator that drives conditional required properties and tells processors how to consume the extension.

version.description: Semver version of the extension itself (not the Formspec version). Within a registry document, the (name, version) tuple MUST be unique. A new major version MAY re-enter draft status.

x-lm.intent: Extension version for uniqueness, ordering, and compatibility resolution.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Transitions: draft → stable → deprecated → retired. Transitions MUST NOT skip states. Stable means the interface is frozen for the major version. Deprecated requires a deprecationNotice. Processors encountering retired extensions SHOULD emit a warning.

x-lm.intent: Lifecycle stage governing processor behavior — warnings for retired, notices for deprecated.

publisher.description: Entry-level publisher override. When present, takes precedence over the document-level publisher for this specific entry. Useful for multi-organization registries.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the extension's purpose and behavior.
examples: US Dollar currency type with 2-digit precision and non-negative constraint.
Returns the federal fiscal year (Oct 1 start) for a given date.

specUrl.description: Link to the full human-readable extension documentation or specification.

schemaUrl.description: Link to a JSON Schema for validating the extension's data as it appears in a Formspec Definition document. Registry-aware processors SHOULD fetch and validate against this schema.

compatibility.description: Version compatibility bounds. Processors MUST verify the current Formspec version satisfies formspecVersion. A mismatch produces a warning (or hard error if x-formspec-strict is set in extensions).

formspecVersion.description: Semver range of compatible Formspec core specification versions. Uses npm-style range syntax.

mappingDslVersion.description: Semver range of compatible Mapping DSL versions. Only relevant for extensions that interact with the Mapping specification.

x-lm.intent: Compatibility bounds that processors MUST check before accepting an extension.

license.description: SPDX license identifier for the extension (e.g. 'Apache-2.0', 'MIT', 'CC0-1.0'). See https://spdx.org/licenses/ for valid identifiers.

deprecationNotice.description: Human-readable deprecation message explaining why the extension is deprecated and identifying a replacement if one exists. REQUIRED when status is 'deprecated'. Processors SHOULD surface this message to form authors.
examples: Use the core money dataType instead. This extension will be retired in v3.0.

examples.description: Array of JSON values demonstrating how this extension is used in a Formspec Definition. Each element is a free-form JSON value — typically a fragment of a definition document or an item using the extension.

extensions.description: Entry-level extension properties for vendor-specific metadata. All property keys MUST be x-prefixed.

baseType.description: The core Formspec data type that this custom dataType extends. REQUIRED when category is 'dataType'. The custom type inherits the base type's serialization, comparison, and FEL operator semantics.

x-lm.intent: Base type inheritance for custom dataType extensions — determines serialization and operator semantics.

constraints.description: Default constraint values applied to fields using this custom dataType. Keys are constraint names, values are their defaults. Only meaningful when category is 'dataType'.

metadata.description: Presentation and descriptive metadata for this entry. For dataType entries, provides rendering hints (prefix, precision, formatting). For concept and vocabulary entries, provides display metadata (displayName). Open object — keys are not restricted.

parameters.description: Function or constraint signature parameters. REQUIRED when category is 'function' or 'constraint'. Each parameter declares name, type, and optional description. Parameters are positional — order matters for function calls in FEL.

name.description: Parameter name used in documentation and error messages.

type.description: Core Formspec data type name for this parameter (e.g. 'string', 'number', 'date', 'boolean', 'array').

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this parameter represents.

x-lm.intent: Function/constraint signature for arity checking and type validation in FEL expressions.

returns.description: Core Formspec data type name for the return value of a custom function. REQUIRED when category is 'function'. Used by processors for type checking in FEL expressions.

x-lm.intent: Return type declaration enabling static type checking of custom function calls in FEL.

members.description: Array of x-prefixed extension names grouped under this namespace. Only meaningful when category is 'namespace'. Members should be other entries in the same or a referenced registry.

conceptUri.description: The concept IRI in the external ontology or standard. REQUIRED when category is 'concept'. This is the globally unique identifier for the concept this entry represents.

x-lm.intent: The globally unique IRI for the concept this registry entry represents.

conceptSystem.description: The ontology or concept system URI. RECOMMENDED when category is 'concept'. Identifies which ontology or standard the concept belongs to.

conceptCode.description: Short code within the concept system (e.g., 'EIN', 'MR'). Only meaningful when category is 'concept'.

equivalents.description: Cross-system equivalences for this concept. Each element declares that the concept is equivalent to a concept in another system, with a SKOS-inspired relationship type. Only meaningful when category is 'concept'.

vocabularySystem.description: The terminology system URI. REQUIRED when category is 'vocabulary'. Identifies the external terminology system this entry represents.

x-lm.intent: Identifies which external terminology system this vocabulary entry represents.

vocabularyVersion.description: Version of the external terminology. Only meaningful when category is 'vocabulary'.

filter.description: Subset constraints limiting which portion of the vocabulary is in scope. Only meaningful when category is 'vocabulary'.

ConceptEquivalent.description: Declares that the bound concept is equivalent to a concept in another system. Relationship types follow SKOS (Simple Knowledge Organization System) semantics.

code.description: The concept code within the target system.

display.description: Human-readable name in the target system.

type.description: Relationship type (SKOS-inspired). When absent, processors MUST treat as 'exact'. Standard values: 'exact' (identical concept), 'close' (very similar), 'broader' (source is more specific), 'narrower' (source is more general), 'related' (associatively related). Custom types MUST be x-prefixed.

VocabularyFilter.description: Constrains which portion of a terminology is in scope for this vocabulary entry.

ancestor.description: Root code for hierarchical filtering. Only descendants of this code are included.

maxDepth.description: Maximum depth from the ancestor code.

include.description: Explicit list of codes to include.

exclude.description: Explicit list of codes to exclude.
